seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service voice agents

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables building sophisticated voice agents by integrating real-time speech-to-text, Azure OpenAI reasoning, and text-to-speech with enterprise-grade security. Voice agents demand sub-second latency and unerring reliability, making guardrails essential to prevent hallucinations, policy violations, and conversation breakdowns. Vex provides runtime verification middleware that ensures responses meet enterprise standards without compromising performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: audio input is transcribed via Azure Speech Service, processed through Azure AI Agent Service for reasoning with grounding (Bing Search, file search, tools), then synthesized back to speech. Azure AI Agent Service orchestrates threads with memory, tools, and Azure OpenAI models while enforcing enterprise policies. The pipeline must maintain <500ms end-to-end latency, requiring careful middleware placement.

Vex integrates as middleware in the agent thread execution loop, validating tool outputs, grounding data freshness, and response safety before TTS. This non-blocking verification preserves latency budgets while catching failures like outdated search results or policy-violating tool calls.

Key components include Agent Threads for conversation state, Tool Calls for enterprise actions, and Grounding Sources (Bing, files) with built-in security controls.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Agents may execute tools that return PII or violate compliance without validation
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware scans tool outputs against enterprise DLP policies before response generation

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Real-time voice conversations fail when agents cite stale search results
risks.mitigation: Vex validates search result recency (<24h) and source credibility before inclusion

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: RAG returns file paths/metadata instead of relevant content excerpts
risks.mitigation: Vex extracts and validates actual content chunks from file search results

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long conversations hit token limits causing abrupt failures mid-response
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers summarization before limits are reached

implementation.title: Real-time STT → Agent Service Pipeline (Python)
implementation.description: Core voice pipeline integrating Azure Speech with Agent Service threads
implementation.code:
import asyncio
import azure.cognitiveservices.speech as speechsdk
from azure.ai.projects import ProjectsClient
from vex_sdk import VexClient

async def voice_agent_pipeline(audio_stream):
    # Speech-to-Text
    speech_config = speechsdk.SpeechConfig(subscription="your-key", region="eastus")
    recognizer = speechsdk.SpeechRecognizer(speech_config=speech_config)
    
    # Agent Service Thread
    client = ProjectsClient(credential=DefaultAzureCredential(), endpoint="https://your-project.api.azure.com")
    thread = await client.agents.create_thread(agent_id="voice-agent")
    
    # Vex Middleware
    vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-key")
    
    result = await recognizer.recognize_once_async(audio_stream)
    if result.reason == speechsdk.ResultReason.RecognizedSpeech:
        # Send to agent with Vex verification
        response = await client.agents.create_message(
            agent_id="voice-agent",
            thread_id=thread.id,
            content=result.text
        )
        verified = await vex.verify_agent_response(response, checks=["latency", "safety"])
        return verified.content
    return None

implementation.title: Latency Budget Monitoring (Python)
implementation.description: Enforce <500ms end-to-end latency with Vex circuit breaker
implementation.code:
import time
from vex_sdk import VexLatencyGuard

def latency_monitored_pipeline(audio_input):
    guard = VexLatencyGuard(budget_ms=500, circuit_breaker_threshold=0.1)
    
    with guard.measure("full_pipeline"):
        start = time.time()
        
        # STT
        stt_result = speech_recognizer.recognize(audio_input)
        
        # Agent Service
        agent_response = agent_client.create_message(
            agent_id="voice-agent",
            content=stt_result.text
        )
        
        # TTS
        audio_out = tts_synthesizer.speak_text(agent_response.content)
        
        latency = (time.time() - start) * 1000
        
        if not guard.check_passed():
            raise Exception("Latency budget exceeded")
    
    return audio_out

vexIntegration.title: Vex Middleware for Agent Service (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Drop-in middleware wrapper for all Agent Service thread operations
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk.integrations.azure import AzureAgentMiddleware
from azure.ai.projects import ProjectsClient

# Wrap existing client
agent_client = ProjectsClient(endpoint="your-endpoint")
vex_middleware = AzureAgentMiddleware(
    client=agent_client,
    api_key="vex-key",
    checks=[
        "enterprise_policy",
        "data_freshness",
        "token_limits",
        "latency_budget"
    ]
)

# All calls automatically verified
response = vex_middleware.create_message(
    agent_id="voice-agent",
    thread_id="thread-123",
    content="user speech input"
)

# Response is guaranteed safe or gracefully degraded
assert response.verified, "Vex blocked unsafe response"

vexIntegration.title: Token Limit Graceful Degradation (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Proactive summarization before token limits are hit
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import TokenGuard

class VoiceTokenGuard(TokenGuard):
    def __init__(self, model="gpt-4o", max_tokens=128000):
        super().__init__(model, max_tokens)
    
    async def check_thread(self, thread_id: str, client):
        thread = client.get_thread(thread_id)
        usage = self.estimate_tokens(thread.messages)
        
        if usage > self.max_tokens * 0.8:
            # Proactive summarization
            summary = await self.summarize_conversation(thread.messages[-10:])
            
            await client.create_message(
                agent_id="voice-agent",
                thread_id=thread_id,
                content=f"[SYSTEM: Conversation summarized to manage token limits] {summary}"
            )
            
        return usage < self.max_tokens

# Usage
guard = VoiceTokenGuard()
if await guard.check_thread("thread-123", agent_client):
    response = agent_client.create_message(...)
else:
    response = "Continuing our conversation about... (context preserved)"
